text: Tend to be huge spenders. (GE) No. (AE) No.
They tend to focus on needs of common people rather than the elite. (GE) Welfare plans and work studies. (AE) No.

Their focus benefits the wealthy more than poor and middle class. (AE) Foreign policy is usually a key issue among candidates. (TM) I think we should take care of our nation first, not spend money on other countries. (AE) No.
Tend to be conservative at spending money. (GE) No. (AE) No.
Perhaps the expression of doing everything with raising the money without raising the debt.

It's the party of the masses of America, the majority who are not the extremely monied. I like just about everything the Dem. Party has tried to do since F.D.R. in its direction towards helping the less privileged--social security, medicare, welfare for the disabled.

I don't feel that they express any honest compassion for any non-monied, less fortunate persons in our society. Those who control the party since 1950 to Eisenhower, express a false patriotism while they would, in honesty, subvert the constitution if they could, for their own aims.
